source: Liang, R., Chen, J., Wu, C., He, K., Wu, Y., Cao, R., Du, R., Zhao, Z., Liu, Y. — "Vulseye: Detect Smart Contract Vulnerabilities via Stateful Directed Graybox Fuzzing", IEEE TIFS 2025.
methodology_note: VulSEye is a stateful directed graybox fuzzer for general smart-contract vulnerabilities — its eval set (per the paper abstract) comprises 4,845 vulnerabilities surfaced in real-world contracts and 11 0-days in the top-50 Ethereum DApps (~2.5M USD impact). The vulnerability classes are general DeFi (reentrancy, access control, arithmetic, etc.), not the cross-chain semantic invariants we benchmark, and the paper does not enumerate the 12 cross-chain bridge incidents in our set. Per-bridge cells therefore stay null with a domain-mismatch note. Aggregate metrics are recorded for context.

polynetwork.note: domain mismatch — VulSEye eval set is general DeFi contracts, not cross-chain bridge incidents

VulSEye does not enumerate per-bridge results because its evaluation domain is general smart-contract fuzzing on 4,845 vulnerabilities + 11 top-50 0-days, not the cross-chain bridge incident set we benchmark.
